title: Jackus Et Jilla.
content:
Jack and Jill
	     Went up the hill,
	 To draw a pail of water;
	     Jack fell down
	     And broke his crown,
	 And Jill came tumbling after.

	        Jackus cum Jill�
	        Formos� ancill�,
	    Aquam hauriturus collem ascendebat;
	        Prolabitur Jackus,
	        Caput miser� fractus,
	    Et Jilla desperata in fatum ruebat.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jacob Bigelow, titled "Jackus Et Jilla.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
